TraceId.description: A unique identifier for this API request. The Hex Support team may request this value when debugging an issue.

hexUserAttributes.description: A map of attributes meant to populate hex_user_attributes in the app for the running user. This must be a string to string map. For more complexity, you can do json serialization

scope.description:
Optionally include additional permissions for a user to do in an embedded app. Valid scopes include:

EXPORT_PDF: allow users to download the app as a pdf
EXPORT_CSV: allow users to download CSVs from tables

inputParameters.description: Optionall sets default values for input states. The keys are the names of the inputs and the values are the default values for those inputs.

expiresIn.description:
Optionally specify the expiration time of the embedding url in milliseconds.
This represents the maximum allowed time between receiving the API response with the single-use signed URL, and requesting the signed URL for the iframe.
Default is 15 seconds. Maximum value is 300 seconds (5 minutes).

showEmbeddedRunButton.description: The showEmbeddedRunButton parameter will ensure the run button always shows up in the embedded app navbar

noEmbedHeaderPadding.description: The noEmbedHeaderPadding parameter removes the top padding added to apps with no tabs.

noEmbedBasePadding.description: The noEmbedBasePadding parameter removes the padding around the embedded app container

noEmbedOutline.description: The noEmbedOutline parameter removes the border around the embedded app

noEmbedFooter.description: The noEmbedFooter parameter removes the Hex-branded footer at the bottom of the embedded app

displayOptions.description: Optionally customize the display of the embedded app

testMode.description: Run the embed API call in test mode. Does not run the app and will not count towards rate limits or embedding usage counts.

ProjectId.description: Unique ID for a Hex project. This can be found in the Variables side bar of the Logic View of a project, or by visiting the Project, and copying the UUID after `hex` in the URL.

ProjectTypeApiEnum.description: The type of the ProjectApiResource being returned.

Timestamp.description: An ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone like 1970-01-01T00:00:00Z.

ScheduleCadenceApiEnum.description: Types of cadence of a schedule for a Hex project.

ResponseRunId.description: Unique ID for a run of a Hex project

ProjectRunStatus.description: Current status of a project run

RunUrl.description: URL to view the current progress of the project run in the Hex UI

ApiPaginationCursor.description:
A cursor string used with `before` and `after` parameters to paginate through a list
of items on the API.

PageSize.description: Number of results to fetch per page for paginated requests

DataConnectionId.description: Unique ID for a data connection.

GroupId.description: Unique ID for a group. This can be found from the groups page (in Settings).

UserId.description: Unique ID for a user. This can be found from the users page (in Settings).

HexSLTypes.PublicProblem.description: A problem encountered during the import of a public HexSL project.

Record_string.Record_string.HexSLTypes.D.description: Construct a type with a set of properties K of type T

SemanticProjectId.description:
Unique ID for a Hex semantic project. This can be found from the semantic
projects admin panel (in Settings).

UpdateSemanticProjectResponse.description: Response body for successful status updates

SemanticObjectUpdate.description:
A single update for a semantic object (dataset or view)
Currently, only status updates are supported.

type.description: The type of object to update

name.description: The name of the dataset or view

status.description: The status name to apply, or null to remove the current status

UpdateSemanticProjectRequest.description:
Request body for updating semantic datasets and views
Currently, only status updates are supported.

Pick_TsoaErrorResponsePayload.Exclude_ke.description: From T, pick a set of properties whose keys are in the union K

Omit_TsoaErrorResponsePayload.details_.description: Construct a type with the properties of T except for those in type K.

name.description: The name of the dataset or view

RunStatusUrl.description: URL to query the status of the project run via the Hex API

ProjectVersion.description: The version of the Hex project that was run. Project versions are ordered, starting at 1 and incrementing on each saved version. Project versions can be found in the History & Versions side bar of the Logic View.

RunNotificationType.description: The condition that determines whether or not the notification should be delivered based on these types.

NotificationRecipientType.description: The delivery type of a notification recipient.

ScreenshotFormatType.description: The file format of the generated screenshot.

id.description: The Id of the recipient. For users and groups, these Ids are found in the settings panel in Hex, while the Id for Slack can be found within the Slack app or URL.

name.description: The human readable name for the user, group, or Slack channel.

isPrivate.description: Whether or not the Slack channel is a private channel in the Slack workspace.

ProjectRunNotificationRecipient.description:
The recipient of a project run notification, which will include the type of recipient and additional configuration details.
This is used to determine who to send the notification to, and what triggers would qualify sending the notification.

includeSuccessScreenshot.description: If true, a screenshot of the project will be included in the notification when the project succeeds.

screenshotFormat.description:
The format of the screenshot to be included in the notification.
Defaults to png only if not specified and includeSuccessScreenshot is true.

ProjectRunResponsePayload.description: Response format returned by the runProject endpoint

NotFoundParam.description: Name of an input parameter passed to the API call that was not found in the project.

dataType.description: The expected data type of the parameter (number, string, etc.), which the input value should have matched

inputCellType.description: The cell input type of the parameter (text, slider, etc.)

paramValue.description: The JSON-encoded, invalid value for the parameter

paramName.description: The name of the parameter for which an invalid value was provided

InvalidParam.description: Information about an input parameter pass to the API call that could not be parsed.

invalid.description: Array of specified input parameters that were invalid or could not be parsed

notFound.description: Array of specified input parameter names that were not found in the project

ProjectRunNotification.description: The configuration details for notifications, which are sent after a run has completed.

includeSuccessScreenshot.description: If true, a screenshot of the project will be included in the notification when the project succeeds.

screenshotFormat.description:
The format of the screenshot to be included in the notification.
Defaults to png if not specified and includeSuccessScreenshot is true.

slackChannelIds.description:
The Slack channels to send the notification to.
The Hex Slackbot must be added to any private channels included.
The Slack Channel Id can be found in the channel's settings from within the Slack app.

userIds.description: The Hex user Ids to send the notification to. These Ids can be found and copied from the Setting's User panel in Hex.

groupIds.description: The Hex group Ids to send the notification to. These Ids can be found and copied from the Setting's Group panel in Hex.

subject.description: An optional message to use as the subject of the notification.

body.description: An optional message to include in the body of the notification.

RunProjectRequestBody.description: Request body format for triggering a project run

inputParams.description:
Optionally specify input parameters for this project run.
These should be structured as a dictionary of key/value pairs, where the key name matches the name of the variable in the Hex project.

Only parameters that are added to the published app can be set via this request parameter. Any additional inputs will be ignored.
It is invalid to pass in both a viewId and inputParams.

If no input parameters are provided, the project will be run with the default input values.
Note that if input parameters are provided, this run will not be able to update the cached values for the project, and the updateCache setting (below) will be ignored.

dryRun.description: When true, this endpoint will perform a dry run that does not run the project. This can be useful for validating the structure of an API call, and inspecting a dummy response, without running a project.

notifications.description:
Optionally specify a list of notification details that will be delivered once a project run completes.
Notifications can be configured for delivery to Slack channels, Hex users, or Hex groups.

updatePublishedResults.description:
When true, the cached state of the published app will be updated with the latest
run results. You must have at least "Can Edit" permissions on the project to do so.
Note: this cannot be set to true if custom input parameters are provided.

useCachedSqlResults.description:
When false, the project will run without using any cached SQL results, and will
update those cached SQL results.

viewId.description:
Optionally specify a SavedView viewId to use for the project run. If specified, the saved view's inputs will be used for the project run.
It is invalid to pass in both a viewId and inputParams.
If not specified, the default inputs will be used.

StateEvent.description: Response format returned by the runProject endpoint

ProjectStatusResponsePayload.description: Response format returned by the getRunStatus endpoint

startTime.description: UTC timestamp of when the project run started

endTime.description: UTC timestamp of when the project run finished

elapsedTime.description: Total elapsed time for the project run in milliseconds

InputRunId.description: Unique ID for a run of a Hex project. This ID is part of the response returned by the RunProject endpoint. The GetProjectRuns endpoint can also be used to find the specific runs for a project.

NextPageUrl.description: URL to fetch the next page of results for a paginated API request

runs.description: Array of run status payloads in the same format returned by the `GetRunStatus` endpoint

LargerPageSize.description: Number of results to fetch per page for paginated requests

UpdateDataConnectionSchemaResponse.description: Response body for successful schema object status updates

SchemaObjectUpdate.description:
Schema object update request - specifies a status change for a database, schema, or table.
Currently, only status updates are supported.

type.description: The type of object to update

name.description: The name of the object - simple for DATABASE, qualified for SCHEMA/TABLE

status.description: The status name to apply, or null to remove the current status

UpdateDataConnectionSchemaRequest.description:
Request body for updating data connection schema object statuses
Currently, only status updates are supported.

value.description: The invalid value (for invalid_status errors) or additional context

OrgGuideFileId.description: Unique ID for a guide file. This can be found by going into the menu of a guide file in the guide files side bar.

id.description: The unique ID of the guide file.

filePath.description:
The file path of the guide file.
example: "guide.md"

id.description: An optional identifier that can be used to distinguish between different unspecified external sources.

repo.description: The name of the github repository.

owner.description: The owner of the github repository.

base.description: The base URL of the github instance. This is usually `https://github.com` (default) unless uploaded from a self-hosted github instance.

branch.description:
The git branch for the uploaded version of the guide file
e.g. `main`

commitHash.description: The git commit hash for the uploaded version of the guide file

path.description:
The path to the guide file within the github repository
e.g. `guides/guide.md`

ExternalSourceInput.description:
A unique identifier for a file that is sourced from an external source.
This is used to track the where the guide file was authored from and where we expect updates to come from.

forceWrite.description:
If true, will overwrite an existing guide that is synced from a different source if there is a guide of the same name
e.g. guide1.md is authored in Hex, request.files includes guide1.md -> this will overwrite the guide that was authored in Hex

default: false

externalSource.description: The external source identifier for the guide file. This is used to track the where the guide file was authored from and where we expect updates to come from.

contents.description: The contents of the guide file. Maximum length is 100000 characters.

filePath.description:
The file path of the guide file.
example: "guide.md"

orgGuideFileIds.description: The IDs of the guides to publish if publishAllGuides is false.

publishAllDraftGuides.description: Publish all draft guides (ignores the orgGuideFileIds field if true)

externalSource.description: The external source that the context changeset is sourced from

ExternalSourceLocatorInput.description: A locator that points to an external source that could include a bunch of context. For example, a github repository

forceWrite.description: If true, will overwrite an existing guide that is synced from a different source if there is a guide of the same name

externalSource.description: The external source that the guide is sourced from

filePath.description: The file path of the guide that will show up in Hex

type.description: Upsert one or more guides against the draft version

externalSource.description: The external source that the guides are sourced from - this is used to figure out which guides Hex expects to be synced and which guides should be deleted

guideFilePaths.description: The file paths of the guides that currently exist in the repo

type.description: Prune guides that are not present in the supplied guidesFilePaths

updateLatestVersion.description: If true, will also update the latest version of the context with the changes from the draft version

CellId.description: Unique ID for a cell. This can be found by going into the menu of a cell in the notebook.

StaticCellId.description: Unique static ID for a cell. This can be found by going into the menu of a cell in the notebook.

runId.description: The run ID. Null when dryRun is true.

runUrl.description: URL to view the run. Null when dryRun is true.

runStatusUrl.description: URL to check run status. Null when dryRun is true.

dryRun.description: True if this was a dry run (validation only, no execution).

info.title: Hex API
info.description: API specification for the Hex External API

post.description: Create an embedded url for a project

Create a new project.

Creates a new project in the workspace with the specified title.
Optionally provide a description and project language.

get.description: List all viewable projects, sorted by most recently created first.
Get metadata about a single project.

patch.description: Use this endpoint to add or remove a status (including endorsements) from a project

get.description: Given a project ID, return the list of warehouse tables queried in the project.

patch.description:
Add a project to collections or remove it from collections.
Workspace tokens calling this endpoint need to have "Collections -> Write access" scope
in addition to "Projects -> Write access" scope.

post.description:
Ingest a semantic project from a zip file.

This API endpoint is subject to a maximum of 3 requests per minute.

verbose.description: Whether to respond with detail on which components of the semantic layer were successfully synced

debug.description: Whether to include additional debug information

dryRun.description: If enabled, the sync will not actually write to the database

patch.description:
Use this endpoint to add or remove a status (including endorsements) from datasets and views within a semantic project

This endpoint uses atomic semantics - if any update in the batch fails validation,
the entire request fails and no changes are applied.

post.description:
Trigger a run of the latest published version of a project.

This API endpoint is subject to a maximum of 20 requests per minute and 60 requests per hour.

get.description: Get the status of the API-triggered runs of a project.
Get the status of a project run.

patch.description:
Use this endpoint to add or remove a status (including endorsements) from databases, schemas, and tables
within a data connection

This endpoint uses atomic semantics - if any update in the batch fails validation,
the entire request fails and no changes are applied

parameters.description:
A JSON stringified, URI encoded external source locator
example: encodeURIComponent(JSON.stringify({"source":"github","owner":"my-org","repo":"my-repo","baseUrl":"https://github.com"}))
parameters.name: externalSource

put.description:
Update or create guide drafts by filePath.

Accepts a dictionary mapping file paths to their contents.
For each file:
- If the guide doesn't exist, it will be created.
- If the guide exists but has no draft, a new draft will be created.
- If the guide exists with a draft, the draft contents will be updated.

get.description:
List all cells

Returns cells from the draft version of the project only, not the published app.
Requires a project id for filtering.
For SQL and CODE cells, includes the source code content.

post.description:
Create a new cell

Creates a new cell in the draft version of a project.
Currently only CODE and SQL cell types are supported.
Requires EDIT_PROJECT_CONTENTS permission on the project.

childPosition.description: Where to place the cell within the parent section. Defaults to LAST.

parentCellId.description: Insert the new cell as a child of a section (parent) cell. Use with childPosition to control placement within the section.

insertAfterCellId.description: Insert the new cell after this cell ID. The cell must belong to the target project.

location.description: Controls where the new cell is placed. If omitted, the cell is appended to the end of the project.

isDataframeSql.description:
Set to true to create a dataframe SQL cell that queries the outputs of other
SQL cells in the project instead of a data connection.
Mutually exclusive with dataConnectionId.

get.description:
Get a single cell by ID

Returns cells from the draft version of the project only, not the published app.
Returns the cell details including id, staticId, cellType, label, dataConnectionId,
source contents (for CODE and SQL cells), and projectId.

patch.description:
Update a cell's source and/or data connection

Updates the source code and/or data connection ID for a cell.
For SQL cells, can update SQL source and data connection ID.
For code cells, can update code source.
Requires EDIT_PROJECT_CONTENTS permission on the project containing the cell.

delete.description:
Delete a cell

Permanently deletes a cell from the draft version of a project.
Requires EDIT_PROJECT_CONTENTS permission on the project containing the cell.
